evidence.fallback_path: rc=64 → docker hard-landing; eventually succeeded via container with HOME bind-mount

root_cause.primary: hexa_remote requires cwd to be under a path mappable on remote (typically $HOME/core/* synced repos); /tmp on Mac (= /private/tmp via symlink) is NOT bind-mounted to remote
root_cause.secondary: rc=64 is sentinel for 'fall through to docker' — by design, but error message doesn't suggest the workaround

concrete_failures.manifestation: every test script user creates in /tmp triggers full failover chain ubu1→hetzner→ubu2→htz before falling to docker (4 ssh probes, ~6-12s wasted)
Mac symlinks /tmp → /private/tmp; cwd is reported as /private/tmp, fails the path-prefix check that expects /Users/ghost/core/*

remediation_options.approach: hexa_remote: detect cwd outside synced roots, skip remote probes entirely, route directly to docker
remediation_options.cost: low — single check before host loop
remediation_options.benefit: eliminates 6-12s of wasted ssh probes for /tmp scripts

remediation_options.approach: auto-resolve symlinks: realpath cwd before mappability check
config-driven sync roots; ~/.hx/sync-roots.json lists prefixes that map to remote
remediation_options.risk: medium — must keep in sync with remote bind-mounts
remediation_options.benefit: user can add custom synced dirs

verdict: ROUTING_FRICTION severity=MEDIUM; ergonomic blocker for ad-hoc testing, well-defined fix
